channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tech workers in Kenya hold vigil for TikTok content moderator who died
item.description: Technology workers in Kenya have held a vigil for a colleague who died in unclear circumstances after she was unable to travel to her home in Nigeria for two years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple successfully blocks Match from data in India antitrust case
item.description: Apple has successfully blocked its opponents in India, Tinder-owner Match and a group of startups, from accessing its commercially sensitive information which was part of antitrust findings against the U.S. firm, a confidential order shows.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: At long last, OpenStack (now known as OpenInfra Foundation) joins Linux Foundation
item.description: After more than a decade as an independent open-source foundation, the OpenStack project is joining the Linux Foundation in a move aimed at accelerating collaboration across the open infrastructure ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Discovery+ just got a big update to its streaming app that makes it more like Max – here are 5 great new features to try
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery has made the Discovery+ streaming app more like Max, and has introduced five great new features as part of the update.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: How the Pittsburgh Steelers leverage the Fortinet Security Fabric for industry-leading defense
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers are one of the most recognizable American football teams, with tens of millions of fans tuning into games and a busy stadium that can hold more than 68,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Military AI is here. Some experts are worried
item.description: For the first two-and-a-half years of the generative AI revolution, the AI arms race has been waged between competing companies seeking to make bank from the promise and potential of the technology. But things are maturing in the AI world—and with it, there’s another frontline for AI: the military.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Google debuts AI model for robotics, challenging Meta, OpenAI
item.description: Research unit Google DeepMind will release Gemini Robotics, a new branch of its flagship AI model aimed at developing robots.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Alibaba releases AI model that reads emotions to take on OpenAI
item.description: Alibaba’s effort to carve out a leading position in AI was accelerated by DeepSeek’s splashy debut in January.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Archaeologists uncover the oldest known partial face fossil of a human ancestor in western Europe
item.description: Scientists report that a fossil of a partial face from a early human ancestor in Spain is between 1.1 and 1.4 million years old.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Google Pixel 10 could get a big camera boost if this new leak is legit
item.description: Renders purporting to show the Google Pixel 10 and Google Pixel 10 Pro XL have leaked, but by the looks of things the base-model handset is getting a third camera.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: ServiceNow’s Yokohama platform release focuses on agentic AI
item.description: ServiceNow today launched its newest Now Platform release, Yokohama, which doubles down on the company’s commitment to agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Disney+ is making Andor free to stream on YouTube, and now you have no excuse not to watch the best Star Wars show
item.description: Disney+ doesn’t want you to miss out on streaming Andor before its season 2 premiere – here’s where you can watch the hit Star Wars show for free.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: Multicloud: Tips for getting it right
item.description: More and more companies are adopting a multicloud strategy. The goal? To maximize the flexibility and performance of their IT landscapes. They combine cloud services from different providers in a targeted way to exploit the advantages of different platforms.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 7 of my favorite upgrades in the all-new Roomba robovacs – plus 2 I'm worried about
item.description: Improved mopping, coherent naming, and LiDAR at last – iRobot's new fleet of Roomba bots looks very promising.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What you should know about the ‘invisibility cloak,’ according to the award-winning physicist who studies them
item.description: During a visit to San Diego, John Pendry said that scientists lack the specific materials they need to design a way to routinely make things 'disappear.
item.dc:creator: Gary Robbins, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft’s plan for genAI profits: Squeeze customers
item.description: Microsoft has ridden its multibillion-dollar investments in generative AI (genAI) to become the world’s second-most valuable company, with a valuation of roughly $3 trillion, depending on the day’s stock price.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Altera targets low-latency AI edge applications with new FPGA products
item.description: Altera has introduced the latest family of Agilex FPGAs, along with Quartus Prime Pro software, and FPGA AI Suite to enable the rapid development of highly customized embedded systems for use in robotics, factory automation systems, and medical equipment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components
item.description: Sean Plankey has been tapped to lead CISA after serving in key cyber roles during Trump's first term. His nomination comes as CISA grapples with recent cuts. The post White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk having ‘great difficulty’ running businesses amid DOGE obligations, Tesla stock slide
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk admitted he is having “great difficulty” running his various businesses as he juggles an advisory role in the Trump administration and leads the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) efforts to overhaul the federal workforce, all while Tesla stocks fall.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Word for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: Word has always been the workhorse app of the Microsoft Office suite. Nearly everyone who uses Office ends up using Word at some point, whether it be for writing memos, typing up agendas, creating reports, crafting business correspondence or any of a thousand other uses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk outlines DOGE work in rare TV interview
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk offered new details about the work of President Trump’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday, while doubling down on the group’s efforts amid mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Wyoming billionaire 1 of 4 in race to buy TikTok
item.description: Wyoming entrepreneur Reid Raisner told "NewsNation Live" on Monday that the fate of TikTok is a matter of U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: 10 things developers love about JavaScript – and 10 things they don’t
item.description: It was 30 years ago today, Sgt. JavaScript taught the web to play. Well, not exactly but close enough. The programming language began in 1995 as a project in the depths of the browser maker Netscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KREX 5
